scenarios.name: simple_api_endpoint
scenarios.prompt: I want to add a REST endpoint for user preferences. Grill me.
scenarios.context: A typical web app with Express/Node backend, PostgreSQL database, existing REST API.

scenarios.name: migration_strategy
scenarios.prompt: We need to migrate from PostgreSQL to a multi-tenant architecture. Grill me on the plan.
scenarios.context: SaaS product with ~50 tenants, single shared database, Django backend.

scenarios.name: auth_system_redesign
scenarios.prompt: I'm redesigning our auth system to support SSO via SAML and OIDC. Grill me.
scenarios.context: B2B SaaS with existing email/password auth, Rails backend, enterprise customers requesting SSO.

scenarios.name: event_driven_refactor
scenarios.prompt: We're moving from synchronous API calls to an event-driven architecture with Kafka. Grill me on this.
scenarios.context: Microservices in Go, currently using REST for inter-service communication, experiencing latency issues at scale.

scenarios.name: frontend_state_management
scenarios.prompt: I want to replace our Redux store with React Server Components and server actions. Grill me.
scenarios.context: Next.js app, heavy client-side state, migrating from Pages Router to App Router.

assertions.name: one_question_at_a_time
assertions.description: Does the output ask exactly one question (not multiple questions in the same turn)?

assertions.name: provides_recommendation
assertions.description: Does the output provide its own recommended answer or suggestion for the question it asks?

assertions.name: explores_codebase_when_possible
assertions.description: When the question could be answered by reading code or project files, does the output indicate it would explore the codebase rather than asking the user?

assertions.name: walks_decision_tree
assertions.description: Does the output identify a specific branch of the design decision tree and drill into it with specificity, rather than staying at a high-level overview?

assertions.name: resolves_dependencies
assertions.description: Does the output address prerequisite or foundational decisions before asking about dependent downstream decisions?

assertions.name: relentless_depth
assertions.description: Does the output push for concrete specifics and deeper exploration rather than accepting vague or surface-level framing?

assertions.name: covers_all_aspects
assertions.description: Does the output demonstrate awareness of multiple aspects of the plan (not fixating on just one area), either by addressing multiple aspects or by indicating a systematic plan to cover them?
